0.q: Under salt stress conditions, the chlorophyll a, chlorophyll b, and total chlorophyll contents in SsNLP7A-overexpressing tomatoes were significantly higher than those in the wild-type.

1.q: The SsHINT1 protein leaves the ER, enters the nucleus and activates SOS1/2/3. After the SOS signal is activated, the expression of related genes is increased, the Na+ content is reduced and the K+ content is increased.

2.q: the Arabidopsis plants overexpressing SsVP showed a tendency to accumulate more Na+ under saline condition than wt plants. The sequestration of Na+ in the vacuole may prevent Na+ toxicity and facilitate cellular K+ uptake, thus, increases salt tolerance of transgenic Arabidopsis plants.

3.q: transgenic tobacco seedlings protected the photosynthesis of tobacco seedlings by closing stomata and protecting PSII reaction centers and electron transport under salt stress

4.q: the overexpression of SsGPAT in Arabidopsis enhances salt tolerance and alleviates the photoinhibition of PSII and PSI under salt stress by improving the unsaturated fatty acid content of PG.

5.q: Motif analysis indicated that the deduced amino acid sequence shows highest homology to the chloroplast precursor polypeptide for subunit VI of the PSI reaction center. Thus, the gene was designated SsPsaH.

6.q: Transgenic Arabidopsis and rice with SsNRT2.5 and its promoter showed enhanced NO3− accumulation, reduced Na+ toxicity, and higher salt tolerance, as well as improved seed NO3− storage and viability compared to WT.

7.q: shoot V-ATPase hydrolytic activity increased dramatically in transgenics when subjected to salt stress treatment, and was nearly twice that of non-transformed controls

8.q: shoot V-ATPase hydrolytic activity increased dramatically in transgenics when subjected to salt stress treatment, and was nearly twice that of non-transformed controls

9.q: both molecular and physiological studies provide strong in vivo evidence that SsHKT1;1 mainly acts as a potassium transporter...involved in salt tolerance by taking part in the maintenance of cytosolic cation homeostasis, particularly, in the maintenance of K+ nutrition under salinity.

10.q: BiFC technology to find out that SsHINT1 and SsNRT1.1C have a protein interaction relationship.

11.q: ultimately confirming the protein interaction relationship between SsHINT1 and SsNRT1.1C; SsNRT1.1C-YFP N + SsHINT1-YFP C results show they interacted.

12.q: SsNRT2.5 expression was significantly upregulated under salt stress and low NO3−–N conditions in S. salsa, which improved NO3− transport in transgenic Arabidopsis thaliana and rice.

13.q: Glycerol-3-phosphate acyltransferase is the first acyl esterifying enzyme in phosphatidylglycerol (PG) synthesis process.

14.q: These results support the idea that Na+ accumulation in SsNHX1-transgenic rice is energized by the H+ electrochemical gradient, particularly by V-ATPase.

15.q: These results support the idea that Na+ accumulation in SsNHX1-transgenic rice is energized by the H+ electrochemical gradient, particularly by V-ATPase.

16.q: SsHKT1;1 mainly acts as a potassium transporter in heterologous expression systems and S. salsa, and that it is involved in salt tolerance by taking part in the maintenance of cytosolic cation homeostasis, particularly the maintenance of K+ nutrition under salinity.
